Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal impairment and/or urinary tract pathology. Low HbA1c may indicate recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Mild metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. Electrolytes are mostly within range, but chloride is slightly elevated. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
